items.myth: Files and installs in Colab persist between sessions.
items.reality: The runtime is ephemeral — local files and pip installs are lost on disconnect; persist to Google Drive.
items.example: Tomorrow you reopen the notebook; the `%pip install langchain` you ran yesterday is gone. Either re-run setup or write to `/content/drive/MyDrive/`.

items.myth: Colab always gives you a powerful GPU for free.
items.reality: GPU/TPU access is subject to quotas and availability; the default runtime is CPU and accelerators can be limited or unavailable.
items.example: After heavy free-tier use, you may see 'Cannot connect to GPU backend' — Google's fair-use quota. Colab Pro / Pro+ guarantee more capacity; the free tier is best-effort.

items.myth: Colab is a different tool from Jupyter.
items.reality: It's a hosted Jupyter environment with the same cell/kernel model plus Google-specific conveniences (Drive, Secrets, managed GPUs).
items.example: Download a Colab notebook as `.ipynb` and open it in local Jupyter — it works (minus the Google-specific cells like `drive.mount` and `userdata.get`).
